---
title: "Projects"
---

A short list of the work I spend the most time on. Each links to a longer page
with the problem, the approach, and where it stands.

---

### West Nile virus thesis — California mosquito surveillance

A quantitative MPH thesis on West Nile virus in California mosquito pools:
modeling infection rate against environmental covariates and surveillance effort,
with spatial and temporal structure handled explicitly. Bayesian hierarchical
spatiotemporal modeling on a county-month analytic grid.

*Spatial epidemiology · Bayesian modeling · surveillance data.* **In progress.**

[Case study →](projects/wnv-thesis.qmd)

---

### MESA — Mapping Epidemiology in Sparse Areas

A C++ desktop application for public-health mapping where data is thin and
population is spread out. Geocoding, spatial clustering, and local-statistic
hot-spot detection (Getis-Ord Gi\*, Moran's I) with Monte Carlo significance
testing, validated against PySAL and R.

*Spatial statistics · desktop software · tool-building.*

[Case study →](projects/mesa.qmd)

---

### FEMA Hazus — GIS and risk-modeling software

Software development in support of FEMA's Hazus natural-hazard loss-estimation
program: GIS-driven hazard and risk workflows, spatial data engineering, database
updates, and validation. C#/C++/SQL Server against ArcGIS-based data.

*GIS software engineering · hazard and risk modeling.*

[Case study →](projects/hazus.qmd)

---

### SEUHD practicum — applied public-health GIS

Practicum work with a local health department: spatial analysis of respiratory
illness using space-time cubes and emerging hot-spot analysis, plus
immunization-compliance reporting built on EHR data and geocoded addresses.

*Applied epidemiology · ArcGIS · surveillance analytics.*

[Case study →](projects/seuhd-practicum.qmd)
